Featured Post The story of who tried to become the center after the web's identity crisis—and what happened when corporations, open-source idealists, and billion-user platforms each claimed the right to vouch for who you are. Microsoft tried monopoly and failed. OpenID built decentralized utopia and nobody used it. Facebook showed up with a blue button and won. The federation wars gave us "Log in with Google" and eliminated password chaos—but at the cost of concentrating identity in an oligopoly that merged authentication with surveillance. The quantum self's multiple personas collapsed into a single, trackable profile optimized for advertising. This is part 5 in the series - The Making of Digital Identity https://lnkd.in/gEMHNSvY The Making of Digital Identity - 05 - The Federation Wars syntheticauth.ai 12 reactions · 3 comments 12 · 3 comments Post The story of how the web gave everyone the power to create themselves from scratch, a hundred times over, on a hundred different sites and how that liberation curdled into a crisis when advertisers, hackers, and the sheer weight of forgotten passwords revealed that a self with no center can't hold. This is part 4 in this series - The Making of Digital Identity https://lnkd.in/gAuVxrUG The Making of Digital Identity - 04 - The Web Identity Crisis syntheticauth.ai 14 reactions · 3 comments 14 · 3 comments Post If the systems verifying who we are are working exactly as designed, what does it mean that the designs are no longer good enough? https://lnkd.in/grVbR5NJ Synthetic Auth Report - Issue # 026 syntheticauth.ai 5 reactions 5 Post We are obsessed with "Who owns the servers?" but we’re ignoring "Who owns the logic?" Europe’s push for digital sovereignty is accelerating, but there’s a deeper layer to the "moat" we’re building. Even if a model is trained in Paris or Berlin, if it’s built on a foundation of data and research from halfway across the world, whose values is it actually optimized for? The Sovereign Illusion: W

section_map.Featured: Featured Post The story of who tried to become the center after the web's identity crisis—and what happened when corporations, open-source idealists, and billion-user platforms each claimed the right to vouch for who you are. Microsoft tried monopoly and failed. OpenID built decentralized utopia and nobody used it. Facebook showed up with a blue button and won. The federation wars gave us "Log in with Google" and eliminated password chaos—but at the cost of concentrating identity in an oligopoly that merged authentication with surveillance. The quantum self's multiple personas collapsed into a single, trackable profile optimized for advertising. This is part 5 in the series - The Making of Digital Identity https://lnkd.in/gEMHNSvY The Making of Digital Identity - 05 - The Federation Wars syntheticauth.ai 12 reactions · 3 comments 12 · 3 comments Post The story of how the web gave everyone the power to create themselves from scratch, a hundred times over, on a hundred different sites and how that liberation curdled into a crisis when advertisers, hackers, and the sheer weight of forgotten passwords revealed that a self with no center can't hold. This is part 4 in this series - The Making of Digital Identity https://lnkd.in/gAuVxrUG The Making of Digital Identity - 04 - The Web Identity Crisis syntheticauth.ai 14 reactions · 3 comments 14 · 3 comments Post If the systems verifying who we are are working exactly as designed, what does it mean that the designs are no longer good enough? https://lnkd.in/grVbR5NJ Synthetic Auth Report - Issue # 026 syntheticauth.ai 5 reactions 5 Post We are obsessed with "Who owns the servers?" but we’re ignoring "Who owns the logic?" Europe’s push for digital sovereignty is accelerating, but there’s a deeper layer to the "moat" we’re building. Even if a model is trained in Paris or Berlin, if it’s built on a foundation of data and research from halfway across the world, whose values is it actually optimized for? The Sovereign Illusion: Who Really Owns Europe's AI Future? Sar Haidar on LinkedIn • 3 min read 17 reactions 17 Post This week, Europe makes its boldest moves yet toward digital sovereignty by replacing American platforms, building its own payment rails, and asserting that a continent's identity shouldn't depend on someone else's infrastructure. Meanwhile, AI barrels ahead: Nature declares machines generally intelligent, AI-generated code overwhelms open source maintainers, Google lets you walk through synthetic worlds, and Waymo's "autonomous" cars turn out to have a human in the Philippines picking their lane. The question that poses itself, is whether you can build a border around a technology that learns from everything, everywhere, all at once? Read this week's issue for hopefully a thoughtful discussion: https://lnkd.in/gK_88Mzp Synthetic Auth Report - Issue # 025 syntheticauth.ai 11 reactions 11 Show all featured items

About “Mediocrity knows nothing but itself; talent instantly recognizes genius.” ✨ I’m a technical recruiter partnering with tier-1, VC-backed AI startups in San Francisco and New York City to help them build their earliest and most critical engineering teams. My focus is on high-bar engineers; builders who thrive in ambiguity, ship fast, and take real ownership. I work closely with founders and engineering leaders at companies backed by top funds to hire senior ICs and early leaders across backend, full-stack, infra, and AI-native roles. What differentiates my work: - Deep exposure to early-stage hiring (Seed → Series C) - A strong signal-to-noise approach: no spray-and-pray recruiting - Clear understanding of what “top-tier” actually means in practice (TT engineers, builder profiles, real ownership & results) - Honest, high-context conversations with candidates about role, expectations, and trajectory I spend most of my time working on searches for: - Senior / Staff Software Engineers - AI Product Engineers - Backend & Infrastructure Engineers - Early Engineering Leaders If you’re an engineer who wants to: ✅ Work directly on core product 👩‍💻 Join a small, elite team 📈 Build in the AI space with real ownership and upside —or a founder looking to hire exceptional technical talent— I’m always open to a conversation. 📩 Feel free to reach out! I'll return your InMail credit :) … more Top skills Technical Recruiting • Stakeholder Management • Talent Sourcing • Negotiation • Sales

section_map.About: About Are you a startup, agency, or business that needs a web app, AI tool, or API built, fast and properly? I'm Abdullah Ijaz, a Freelance Full Stack Developer & AI Engineer based in Lahore (available worldwide, remote-first). I turn ideas into production-ready digital products using modern tech stacks. What I build for clients: → Full-stack web applications (React, Next.js, Node.js, Flask) → AI-powered features chatbots, automation pipelines, OpenAI & Gemini API integrations → Backend systems, REST APIs, and database architecture (PostgreSQL, MongoDB, MySQL) → Payment gateway integrations (Stripe, JazzCash) → Mobile-responsive, SEO-ready frontends (Tailwind CSS, Bootstrap) Why clients work with me: → Clean, documented, maintainable code → Fast delivery with regular progress updates → End-to-end ownership,I handle frontend, backend, database, and deployment → 2+ years freelancing on Fiverr & Upwork with repeat clients Tech Stack: Python · Flask · FastAPI · React · Next.js · TypeScript · Node.js · PostgreSQL · MongoDB · TensorFlow · OpenAI API · Docker · REST APIs Let's talk if you need: A developer who ships quality work on time, communicates clearly, and takes full ownership of your project. 📩 DM me or email: abdullahijazdeveloper@gmail.com 🔗 GitHub: github.com/ijazabdullah127 … more Top skills Full-Stack Development • MERN Stack • MongoDB • React.js • Node.js

section_map.Projects: Projects Velora: High-Performance Hybrid Screen Recorder (Desktop & Web) Apr 2026 – Apr 2026 Associated with Upwork Velora is a professional-grade, performance-first screen recording ecosystem designed to bridge the gap between simple web recorders and heavy desktop software. Key Technical Achievements & Contributions: Hybrid Native Engine: Developed a dual-path processing system that detects the user's environment to deliver maximum performance. On Desktop, it utilizes a custom IPC Bridge to a native FFmpeg engine, achieving video conversion speeds 5x–10x faster than standard browser-based WASM implementations. Advanced Video Compositing: Built a canvas-based compositor that blends multiple high-definition streams (Screen + Webcam) in real-time with pixel-perfect accuracy and minimal CPU overhead. Selective Capture Logic: Engineered a premium window-selection interface for the Windows version, allowing users to record specific applications with high-fidelity frame rates (60FPS) while bypassing common browser tab-throttling issues. Privacy-Centric Architecture: Designed the app to be "local-first," ensuring all video data is processed directly on the user's hardware, providing zero-latency recording and 100% data security. AI Integration: Integrated MediaPipe for advanced Selfie Segmentation, allowing users to apply professional virtual backgrounds and dynamic PiP overlays. The result: A cinematic recording experience that is completely free, watermark-free, and enterprise-ready. … more React, TypeScript and +2 skills Formula 1 Data Analytics Aug 2025 – Oct 2025 Associated with Fiverr Formula 1 Data Analytics Capstone Project is an advanced end-to-end data science initiative designed to uncover key determinants of success in Formula 1 racing. Spanning 70+ years of F1 history (1950–2025), this project integrates large-scale data analysis, predictive modeling, and real-time dashboards to deliver actionable insights into driver performance, race strategies, and team reliability. The workflow covers full-stack analytics — from raw data extraction via OpenF1, FastF1, and F1DB, to model training and deployment using Python, XGBoost, and Streamlit. Key features include: 🧠 Predictive Models: Race outcomes, podium finishes, and DNF probabilities (96%+ accuracy). 📈 Interactive Dashboard: 8 pages built with Streamlit for live predictions, performance evaluation, and circuit exploration. 🌦️ Weather Integration: Real-time weather and temperature data for race outcome correlation. 🗺️ Circuit Maps: Interactive global visualization of 22+ F1 circuits using Folium. 🚗 Live Season Tracker: Automated 2025 season updates using FastF1 API. ⚙️ Feature Engineering: 100+ engineered variables across driver, constructor, and circuit performance. 🔍 Model Explainability: SHAP, LIME, and Optuna for transparency and optimization. section_map.Projects: Projects 3D Map Generation Aug 2024 – Nov 2024 Associated with Indraprastha Institute of Information Technology, Delhi Project Title: 3D Map Generation of a City Using Machine Learning Techniques Description: This project focuses on developing efficient methods to generate high-quality 3D city maps by integrating machine learning with data from aerial imagery, LiDAR scans, and terrain points. The objective is to create accurate terrain models, detailed building reconstructions, and robust spatial topology representations. Techniques like triangulation, fractal algorithms, and Delaunay Triangulation are combined with ML for automation, noise reduction, and feature extraction. Key Outcomes: - Realistic 3D maps with high geometric precision and visual fidelity. - Efficient methods suitable for large-scale urban datasets. - Applications in urban planning, VR/AR, disaster management, and geospatial simulations. This project offers a scalable, innovative solution for creating realistic 3D city maps, enhancing usability for real-world and research applications. … more Machine Learning, 3D Modeling and +5 skills Other contributors

section_map.About: About At Wizphys AI, my team and I revolutionized physiotherapy assistance, leveraging deep learning to enhance real-time solutions and user retention by 74%. My role as the lead AI engineer entailed innovating beyond conventional models, optimizing algorithms for edge device deployment, and pushing the boundaries of exercise accuracy. As a Master's candidate at Stevens Institute of Technology, I am expanding my knowledge in applied artificial intelligence, driving my passion for developing AI applications that deliver significant health benefits and technical innovation in the healthcare sector. My academic pursuits complement my practical expertise, underscoring a commitment to delivering impactful user experiences. … more Top skills Machine Learning • Python (Programming Language) • Deep Learning • Natural Language Processing (NLP) • Computer Vision
section_map.Experience: Experience Machine Learning Engineer Conduit · Internship Sep 2025 - Jan 2026 · 5 mos San Francisco Bay Area · Remote Designed and deployed a scalable computer vision manufacturing process monitoring system to improve Overall Equipment Effectiveness (OEE). Built and deployed an end-to-end machine utilization pipeline using YOLO (object detection), including model evaluation, benchmarking, and performance validation. Fine-tuned and deployed PaddleOCR on edge devices for CNC HMI screen text recognition, enabling real-time data ingestion via MQTT to backend servers. … more Computer Vision, Machine Learning and +4 skills Research Assistant Stevens Institute of Technology · Apprenticeship Jan 2025 - Aug 2025 · 8 mos Hoboken, New Jersey, United States · On-site Conducted research on gait parameter estimation, focusing on data cleaning & model development. Trained a transformer model to estimate the center of pressure (COP) from insole sensor, accelerometer, and gyroscope data, and compared its performance with GPR and LSTM models for mitochondrial disease assessment. https://www.wrslab.com/ … more Research, Gait Analysis and +3 skills Machine Learning Engineer Wizphys AI pvt ltd · Full-time Mar 2022 - Jun 2024 · 2 yrs 4 mos Pune District, Maharashtra, India · On-site As the lead AI engineer at Wizphys AI, I have been an integral part of the company since its inception, contributing significantly to the development and success of our physiotherapy assistance app. 1) Spearheaded the end-to-end development of the ML stack, from data collection to edge device model deployment, ensuring a robust foundation for real-time physiotherapy assistance. 2) Introduced an innovative approach to improve accuracy in exercises with minimal movement. This involved devising solutions beyond conventional pose detection models, providing our app with a unique advantage. This improved user retention by 74%. 3) Implemented model optimization techniques (quantization, pruning) to enable efficient deployment on diverse devices, including low-end mobile phones, ensuring universal accessibility and real-time audio prompts, all while prioritizing user privacy through on-device inference without data leaving the user's phone. Improved 10 FPS to 30 FPS. 4) Implemented an automated data collection and model training pipeline, ensuring continuous improvement in model accuracy over time. 5) Engineered a comprehensive logic system for 21 shoulder exercises, addressing potential errors and enhancing the accuracy of user movements during sessions. 6) Designed an intuitive app architecture for optimal user experience, making exercises accessible to users of all skill levels and age groups. 7) Led and mentored a team of four ML engineers, enabling rapid deployment of new features and improvements every week. … more ‎Wizio - AI Physio Coach Design Review, Management and +17 skills Computer Vision Engineer ModakaTech | Camweara · Full-time Jul 2021 - Jan 2022 · 7 mos Bangalore Urban, Karnataka, India Worked in a small team of smart individuals to develop, debug and maintain web-based solutions for virtual try-on. Major projects I worked upon: 1) Trained and deployed a custom jewelry segmentation deep learning model to eradicate the need for third-party applications from our pipeline. 2) Integrated selfie-segmentation model with current Try-on feature for face products. 3) Debugged various issues in the Try-on project to maintain a smooth user experience for clients. 4) Worked on a custom hand landmark detection DL model built using TensorFlow to improve the accuracy of the ring product's alignment with the finger. … more Management, Oral Communication and +12 skills Product Developer - R&D Om Siddh Vinayak Impex Pvt Ltd · Full-time Jul 2020 - Jul 2021 · 1 yr 1 mo Gandhidham, Gujarat, India My role involved contriving smart solutions with a pragmatic approach to resolving practical barriers that hinder the performance and quality KPIs. My Role majorly involves benchmarking and fine-tuning the Perception Algorithms and the Hardware Accelerators for effective deployment on the server as well as on edge devices. Some major Projects include Automatic Number Plate Recognition ( with a custom trained DNN OCR ), Smart collection box, Vision-based Automatic laser cutting machine ( Image segmentation ), Smart Switch (object detection and tracking), Smart street lights, Automatic Face Attendance System. Actively involved in building all the projects mentioned above right from initial inception of designing, building and deploying. Managing the whole lifecycle under sharp deadlines. Proficient in Object detection, Object tracking, Instance segmentation, OCR, Nvidia's DeepStream, image processing, OpenCV, CUDA, Tensorflow, Pytorch, Keras, 3D point cloud, reconstruction, Tkdnn, SQLite, MongoDB, various IoT sensors, static LIDAR. … more Design Review, Oral Communication and +9 skills Show all

section_map.About: About I am a Software Engineer and MSCS student at Stony Brook University with experience in AI/ML, GenAI, distributed systems, cloud infrastructure, and fintech applications. I’m passionate about FinTech, product development, and startup ecosystems, where I can apply my skills to build products that are reliable, explainable, and impactful. About AI Engineer and Software Developer with hands-on experience building intelligent systems using Generative AI, Large Language Models (LLMs), NLP, and Machine Learning. I specialize in developing end-to-end AI solutions that combine data, backend systems, and modern AI frameworks to solve real-world problems. Currently working as an AI Engineer at Solugenix, where I design and develop AI-driven applications including LLM-based chatbots, data analysis systems, and automation tools. My work involves integrating technologies such as Python, LangChain, OpenAI APIs, and vector databases to build scalable AI solutions capable of analyzing datasets, generating insights, and supporting intelligent decision-making. I have practical experience building projects such as: • AI-powered chatbot systems that analyze CSV datasets and generate insights and visualizations • Code analysis and documentation generation tools using LLMs • Automated test case generation systems for Java applications • Data-driven AI assistants capable of querying structured data from databases like PostgreSQL I’m always interested in exploring innovative applications of AI, especially in Generative AI, intelligent automation, and data-driven systems that can create real impact for businesses and users. Apart from that I love reading and have been practicing Transcendental Meditation for more than 10 years. … more

section_map.Featured: Featured Post Multi-Agent E-Commerce Intelligence System: Intelligent Assistance for Online Shoppers ✨ Excited to share my latest AI project a Multi-Agent E-Commerce Intelligence System designed to solve one of the biggest problems in online shopping: information overload and decision fatigue. 🛒 Today, users browse hundreds of products, read scattered reviews, and remain unsure about what to buy. To address this, I built a modular multi-agent AI workflow that acts like a personal shopping assistant. 🚀 What the System Does It uses four intelligent agents working together: 1️⃣ Product Retrieval Agent Fetches relevant products using keyword matching 2️⃣ Sentiment Analysis Agent Analyzes customer reviews in batches using Gemini AI Extracts pros, cons, positives & negatives 3️⃣ Question Answering Agent Uses LLM reasoning to answer user questions using context 4️⃣ Recommendation Agent Rule-based system suggesting the best product + alternatives All agents are coordinated by a Multi-Agent Orchestrator that runs sequential + parallel workflows for speed & accuracy. 💡 Sample Use Case User query: “Which laptop is best for video editing under $1000?” ✔ Retrieves all laptops ✔ Summarizes reviews (sentiment) ✔ Answers user-specific questions ✔ Gives final recommendation + alternatives ➡️ Result: A complete, expert-like shopping guidance experience. 🧠 Tech Stack Python 3.8+ ADK, Google Generative AI (Gemini 1.5 Flash) for sentiment & Q/A NumPy, Pandas for processing 🔭 What’s Next Implement orchestration using LangChain Deploying with FastAPI Adding Redis caching Moving to Pinecone for semantic search Building a simple React UI If you want to look deeper into the full implementation, here is the GitHub link: 👉 Source Code: https://lnkd.in/gmPgnNH6 27 reactions · 1 comment 27 · 1 comment Link RAG - Chatbot GitHub Developed an AI chatbot using LLaMA-2, LangChain, and Chroma for FAQ answering. Implemented a Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) pipeline for accurate responses. Utilized CSV-based knowledge base with vector embeddings. Deployed via Streamlit using free Colab / Hugging Face resources. Used regex for simple patterns and Sentence Transformers for embeddings. Applied Logistic Regression for structured logs and LLM for unstructured logs. Improved overall classification accuracy across multiple log types. Skills LLM, Sentence Transformers Regex, Logistic Regression Python, Scikit-learn Check-it out - https://github.com/avrashmi/Log_Classification Media Animal Detection Using Machine Learning Developed an object detection pipeline for African wildlife classification. Trained YOLOS-tiny model using Hugging Face Transformers. Used custom-labeled YOLO dataset for detection and evaluation. Achieved high accuracy for multiple species detection. section_map.Projects: Projects Animal Detection Using Machine Learning Associated with Trent University This project implements an object detection pipeline to automatically detect and classify African wildlife (Buffalo, Elephant, Rhino, and Zebra) using deep learning. It leverages transformers, PyTorch, and Hugging Face’s YOLOS-tiny model for efficient detection and training on custom datasets. The workflow includes: Dataset Preparation – Images and YOLO-style bounding box annotations of animals (Zebra, Buffalo, Rhino, Elephant). Data Processing – Custom PyTorch Dataset class for reading, resizing, and normalizing images. Model Training – Fine-tuning Hugging Face’s YOLOS-tiny object detection transformer using PyTorch Lightning. Evaluation – Using Mean Average Precision (mAP) metrics at multiple IoU thresholds. Visualization – Bounding box predictions drawn on sample images for visual inspection. Results The model predicts bounding boxes and labels for Buffalo, Elephant, Rhino, Zebra. Evaluation uses Mean Average Precision (mAP) at IoU thresholds (0.5, 0.75, etc.). Visualization confirms accurate localization and classification on test data. … more Machine Learning, PyTorch and +3 skills RAG-based Chatbot Built an AI-powered FAQ assistant using LLaMA-2, LangChain, Chroma, and Streamlit. section_map.About: About I am a passionate and dedicated individual with a unique blend of experiences that have shaped both my personal and professional journey. Early in my life, I demonstrated an entrepreneurial spirit by buying my family their first computer at the age of 14 when I got my work permit. During my summer breaks, I diligently worked to support my family, and by the age of 16, I was not only using the computer but also taking it apart and upgrading its components. My fascination with technology led me to establish a small business at the age of 18, specializing in setting up home and business networks. By the time I turned 21, I was immersed in selling and building custom PC and laptop orders tailored to meet the unique needs of each customer. I am a proud gay Latino nerd seeking my professional home as a Front-end engineer or in an IT help desk role, drawing from my background as a graduate of General Assembly and former engineering manager at Bitwise Industries. Despite facing personal challenges, including a diagnosis of ADHD and depression, I've found strength in my identity and a passion for breaking into the Tech Industry. Running serves as my solace during stressful times, while video games offer a delightful escape. In my previous role as an engineering manager, I observed distinct differences in the performance and dynamics of my team, consisting of 11 individuals. Their commitment and trust were evident, as they willingly tackled challenging projects and approached me with confidence about important matters. My holistic management style involves effectively addressing individual needs, removing obstacles, and fostering trust, resulting in the thriving development of team members. The following recommendations on my LinkedIn profile attest to my leadership skills. Before my tech journey, I spent seven years in hospitality, including four years in a leadership position, cultivating transferable skills such as project management, effective communication, empathy, office procedures, and delivering constructive feedback. https://calendly.com/gutierrezjoshuah … more Top skills Mentoring • Front-end Development • People Management • React.js • MongoDB
section_map.Featured: Featured Link Joshuah's Portfolio Site https://portfolio-fz3t.onrender.com Displays my body of work. Made using React and deployed on Render.com Link Udemy Course Completion Certificate Udemy Post Just finished the course “Having Difficult Conversations: A Guide for Managers”! #interpersonalcommunication #peoplemanagement 1 reaction 1 Post 🌟 Reflecting on the Power of Management Styles 🌟 How do I know my management style works with only 2+ years in the industry? Greetings, LinkedIn community! I'm Joshuah Gutierrez, a passionate professional based in Burlingame, CA, with a drive to make a difference in the tech industry. Today, I want to share a bit about my journey, as well as my management philosophy that has yielded remarkable results. As a proud member of the LGBTQ+ community, I believe in embracing our unique experiences and backgrounds, rather than hiding them. It is through these experiences that we can genuinely connect and empathize with those around us. Combining this belief with my battles against ADHD, depression, and my ongoing commitment to sobriety, I've gained a deeper understanding of resilience, compassion, and the value of diversity. In my previous role, I had the privilege of leading a team of 11 exceptional individuals. What set my team apart was their unwavering commitment, trust, and ability to consistently outperform other teams in our department. With open lines of communication, my team effortlessly provided timely updates, eliminating unnecessary follow-ups and streamlining our workflow. They fearlessly embraced complex projects typically reserved for senior positions and approached me with confidence, knowing they would be heard and supported. My management style can best be described as holistic. I firmly believe that by actively managing individuals, removing obstacles from their paths, building trust, and addressing their unique needs, we unlock their full potential. Witnessing the thriving success of developers under my guidance has reinforced my confidence in this approach. But don't just take my word for it! I encourage you to visit my LinkedIn profile and explore the glowing recommendations shared by my team members and colleagues. Their heartfelt testimonials truly speak to the impact of my management style. Together, let's foster inclusive workplaces where diverse backgrounds are celebrated, mental health is prioritized, and everyone's voices are heard. Feel free to connect with me, and let's embark on this journey of growth and success! #ManagementStyle #DiversityandInclusion #EmployeeEngagement #Leadership #TechIndustry #LinkedInCommunity 5 reactions · 1 comment 5 · 1 comment Post Just finished the course “Be the Manager People Won't Leave”! #peoplemanagement 3 reactions 3

profiles.seed_headline: Front-end Developer 🏳️‍🌈 Holistic Engineering Manager - React Team Manager
profiles.about: About I am a passionate and dedicated individual with a unique blend of experiences that have shaped both my personal and professional journey. Early in my life, I demonstrated an entrepreneurial spirit by buying my family their first computer at the age of 14 when I got my work permit. During my summer breaks, I diligently worked to support my family, and by the age of 16, I was not only using the computer but also taking it apart and upgrading its components. My fascination with technology led me to establish a small business at the age of 18, specializing in setting up home and business networks. By the time I turned 21, I was immersed in selling and building custom PC and laptop orders tailored to meet the unique needs of each customer. I am a proud gay Latino nerd seeking my professional home as a Front-end engineer or in an IT help desk role, drawing from my background as a graduate of General Assembly and former engineering manager at Bitwise Industries. Despite facing personal challenges, including a diagnosis of ADHD and depression, I've found strength in my identity and a passion for breaking into the Tech Industry. Running serves as my solace during stressful times, while video games offer a delightful escape. In my previous role as an engineering manager, I observed distinct differences in the performance and dynamics of my team, consisting of 11 individuals. Their commitment and trust were evident, as they willingly tackled challenging projects and approached me with confidence about important matters. My holistic management style involves effectively addressing individual needs, removing obstacles, and fostering trust, resulting in the thriving development of team members. The following recommendations on my LinkedIn profile attest to my leadership skills. Before my tech journey, I spent seven years in hospitality, including four years in a leadership position, cultivating transferable skills such as project management, effective communication, empathy, office procedures, and delivering constructive feedback. https://calendly.com/gutierrezjoshuah … more Top skills Mentoring • Front-end Development • People Management • React.js • MongoDB

section_map.About: About Heralding from Nashville, I am of course, the stereotypical musician with a real day job. I'm currently a freelance consultant who is consumed by the fast-moving world of agentic AI engineering. Professionally for 20 years, I have worked as a general technologist. Started with economics and quantitative finance, then data science and machine learning, and then landing on building data-intensive web and hybrid mobile applications. In the evolving age of AI, code is increasingly trivial, but experienced insight is not. I leverage many years worth of complex product challenges into enduring solutions. As models inevitably get better, so should your teams, and so should your products. Prioritizing security, strict type safety, exhaustive build-time validation, I ensure that when your teams use AI agents to scale development, they have a clear and efficient self-correcting framework to operate. I help launch or grow companies and non-profit organizations as a fractional CTO or as an senior+ IC. My preferred tech stack is E2E TypeScript with React and its meta-frameworks (Next.js) or React Native. … more
section_map.Experience: Experience Principal Consultant sscotth.io · Freelance Nov 2013 - Present · 12 yrs 6 mos Nashville Metropolitan Area · Remote Principal consultant for an independent technology consultancy working across a wide range of languages and technologies focused on AI enablement, strategies, and other technical leadership for new or existing web/mobile solutions. Roles: · Support in-house teams, startups, and other consultancies as a impediment remover. Acting as an outside observer I focus heavily on understanding user problems and shipping fast solutions. Wearing many hats as needed but most comfortably as a lead contributor, consulting architect, or fractional manager/CTO. · Continually and proactively convert ambiguous concepts both technical and non-technical from end-to-end e.g. identifying business strategy, securing stakeholder buy-in/investment, defining incremental technical roadmaps, and crafting execution. Offerings and Experiences: · Performant, type-safe full stack web development using scalable serverless platforms (Next.js/Vite/React). · Automated testing across the stack, FE (Cypress/Playwright/Storybook), BE (Jest/Mocha/Chai/Postman), and Mobile (Detox). · Data orchestration with event-driven ETL DAG pipelines with various tools such as Airflow, Kestra, dbt. · AI agent integrations including RAG search, NLP classification, data extraction, and handling realtime event-stream APIs (MLFlow, PyTorch, n8n). · Infrastructure as code (IaC) with AWS (CDK/CloudFormation/Pulumi/SST). · Complying with various privacy standards such as HIPAA, TIPA (Tennessee Information Protection Act), COPPA (Children's Online Privacy Protection Rule), CCPA (California Consumer Privacy Act), and GDPR (EU's General Data Protection Regulation). · Security audits of code and dependencies with CVE notifications, insights, and attention. · Non-exhaustive list of primary technologies include TypeScript, React, Next.js (RSC), React Native, Python, AWS See a few notable example projects here[1] [1]: https://www.linkedin.com/in/sscotth/#projects … more Lead Instructor (Full Stack Web Development) Nashville Software School · Full-time May 2014 - Mar 2017 · 2 yrs 11 mos Nashville Metropolitan Area · On-site Solo full-stack web development instructor for classes and 1-on-1 mentoring to over 250 students, teaching assistants, and junior instructors at a non-profit six-month full-time immersive developer bootcamp. section_map.About: About I’ve spent 15+ years launching innovative tech products and scaling teams that turn strategy into real-world impact. From data wrangling jet engine and satellite systems to building speech analytics platforms and generative AI tutors. Highlights Deep is a new connection “Hi Deep, it’s great to connect with you. Looking forward to staying in touch.” Say hello

About I design and deploy security systems that operate at the platform level — securing cloud infrastructure, AI-driven workflows, and software supply chains before vulnerabilities reach production. My focus is not just finding issues, but engineering security into systems so they fail safely by default. I’ve built and shipped: • LLM-powered vulnerability triage system processing 500+ daily signals, reducing analyst triage time by ~40% and prioritizing exploitable risks • Distributed fuzzing infrastructure on Kubernetes (AFL++), increasing coverage by ~60% and enabling continuous memory-safety testing • SLSA Level 2-compliant container pipeline with SBOM + vulnerability gating — achieving 100% image coverage with zero critical CVEs reaching staging • Policy-as-code security guardrails (OPA + Terraform) enforcing secure defaults across CI/CD pipelines with zero high-severity misconfigurations reaching production • IAM detection and enforcement system identifying privilege escalation risks with 95%+ accuracy across multi-service environments I work across the intersection of: Platform Security · Cloud Security · AI/LLM Security · Supply Chain Security · Detection Engineering Tech Stack: Kubernetes · eBPF · OPA · Terraform · SIEM (ELK) · Trivy · Sigstore · Python · Go · AFL++ … more Top skills Security Engineering • Cloud Security • Machine Learning Security Operations (MLSecOps) • Product Security • Security Automation

section_map.Featured: Featured Post Still in the development phase, here's the current status of ARGUS. ARGUS is a SOAR platform being built from the ground up. What's working today: - Alert ingestion from 12 security tools - Real-time WebSocket feed (live, not mocked) - MITRE ATT&CK mapping with confidence scoring - 8 automated response playbooks - JWT authentication, Role-Based Access Control, Docker deployment What I'm still wiring up: - Persistent database layer (migrating off in-memory store) - Webhook integrations for real tool APIs - Alert deduplication engine - Mobile-responsive layout Every day, it gets closer to being production-grade. This project is designed for a scale where failure has significant consequences, and the architecture must be robust. #SecurityEngineering 53 reactions 53 Post In financial systems, security failures often manifest subtly and systemically rather than loudly. In transaction-heavy environments, it becomes clear that risk originates not only from external attackers but also from weak service boundaries, overly broad permissions, and assumptions embedded in integrations. In financial platforms, even minor design decisions can lead to significant consequences. Here are a few practices that consistently matter: - Enforcing strict least-privilege identity and access management (IAM) between services that handle transaction or customer data. - Treating internal APIs as hostile by default, implementing authentication, authorization, and rate limiting. - Logging with intent by designing telemetry that supports fraud detection and forensic traceability. Security in financial systems is not merely about adding controls everywhere; it is about constructing predictable, auditable, and resilient architectures from the outset. For those involved in fintech or high-trust environments, what architectural decision has had the most significant impact on reducing systemic risk? 11 reactions 11 Post Most security breakdowns do not stem from a lack of tools; they arise from systems that generate more alerts than understanding. In various cloud and application environments, a consistent pattern has emerged: scanners produce volume rather than clarity, duplicated findings obscure real risks, and critical vulnerabilities surface too late to be effective. The issue lies not in the effort put forth, but in the signal flow. This architecture is specifically designed to address that gap. In practice, this approach: - Reduces vulnerability triage time by 40% - Surfaces memory-safety issues traditional scanners miss - Embeds security directly into CI/CD and Kubernetes rather than adding late-stage gates I’m sharing this to contribute to the broader security engineering community—because scalable security isn’t about adding more tools; it’s about designing better signal flow. The best security systems are boring when they work—because nothing breaks. If you’re building or securing cloud platforms, I’d love to hear how you’re tackling signal vs noise. #SecurityEngineering #CloudSecurity #DevSecOps #ProductSecurity #SecurityAsCode 10 reactions 10

Ruby on Rails Developer with 5 years of experience building scalable SaaS platforms, API-driven systems, and production-grade web applications. My work focuses on designing reliable backend systems that handle real-world complexity - third-party integrations, background job processing, payment workflows, IoT event pipelines, and compliance-driven data systems. I actively leverage AI-assisted development workflows and structured prompt engineering to improve debugging efficiency, test coverage, documentation clarity, and overall development velocity. Over the past few years, I’ve built and maintained systems including: • Real-time webhook processing pipelines for IoT emergency response platforms • HubSpot CRM synchronization architecture using Sidekiq with retry-safe background jobs • Stripe payment workflows with transaction reliability and failure handling • Financial compliance systems generating government-compliant XML/XLS reports • Role-based SaaS platforms built on scalable REST API architecture • Automated database backup and production deployment workflows My engineering focus includes: • Clean service-layer architecture • Database performance optimization and query tuning • Idempotent background jobs and resilient async workflows • System reliability, monitoring, and failure handling • Writing maintainable and scalable backend systems Tech Stack: Ruby, Ruby on Rails (5/6/7/8), Sidekiq, PostgreSQL, MySQL, REST APIs, Webhooks, AWS, Linux, Nginx I also bring practical full-stack experience, building admin dashboards and user-facing features within Rails applications using HTML, CSS, and JavaScript. Currently exploring product engineering roles where I can contribute to scalable systems and continue growing in system design and distributed architecture. Let’s connect. … more

section_map.About: About I'm a Full-Stack AI Engineer based in Paris, currently building a Safety & Compliance MVP at Siloett.AI (Station F). My work sits at the intersection of applied AI, cloud data engineering, and natural language processing — and I thrive in fast-paced startup environments where I can wear multiple hats and ship meaningful products. My technical toolkit includes designing Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) systems, building AI agents, architecting cloud-based data pipelines on Microsoft Azure, and fine-tuning large language models. At Floware, I was the sole data scientist and cloud engineer in a team of four, where I built the entire batch-processing data architecture from scratch — streamlining mobility data workflows and delivering measurable cost savings. Before that, I spent two years in NLP research. At DiCE Lab (Paris), I independently proposed and developed a fine-tuned translation model for low-resource languages, and at AIT BrainLab (Bangkok), I helped build a paraphrasing tool and improved model accuracy by 15% through better data cleaning and training pipelines. I hold two Master's degrees in Data Science — one from Télécom SudParis (Institut Polytechnique de Paris) and one from the Asian Institute of Technology. I've also completed specializations in Machine Learning (DeepLearning.AI / Stanford), Python (University of Michigan), and Agile Development (IBM). What sets me apart is a less conventional path: before data science, I worked in advertising and international development (WorldFish/CGIAR), which sharpened my communication, storytelling, and cross-cultural collaboration skills — abilities that make me a stronger teammate and a more thoughtful problem-solver. I'm currently open to opportunities as an AI Engineer, Data Scientist, Data Engineer, or ML Engineer. If you're building something interesting, I'd love to connect. 📫 Feel free to reach out — let's talk AI, data, or collaboration. … more

section_map.About: About I'm a computational linguist dedicated to building technology that is culturally coherent, institutionally resilient, and ethically verifiable. I ask: What would it mean to design Al systems accountable not just to metrics, but to the communities, ecosystems, and languages they shape? Through the Linguistic Security Institute (LSI), I develop frameworks for Al governance, linguistic risk assessment, and community-centered design. My work spans: 🔹 Error analysis & model evaluation - Identifying bias, "Clever Hans" effects, and failure patterns across LLMs 🔹 Multilingual data QA - Gold label auditing, annotation, and quality frameworks for voice Al 🔹 Open-source collaboration - Contributing to Arabic NLP with the NAMAA community 🔹 Speaking & dialogue - University symposia, security conferences, and international forums 🔹 Let’s connect! I am always open to conversations about Al governance, linguistic security, and building technology that gives back. I'm particularly interested in connecting with professionals and mentors working where language, security, and governance intersect, especially in ethical AI, culturally informed tech, or policy for multilingual systems. Check out my projects on GitHub: github.com/bintdamiana I’d love to hear your story and explore collaboration opportunities! … more Top skills Team Leadership • Project Management • Multilingual Communication
section_map.Featured: Featured Post Language is becoming an attack vector. As a computational linguist, I may not have a traditional technical background but I value being a builder. The cybersecurity community (& BSides San Diego) has welcomed me anyway. You showed me that security isn't just about code. It's about communication and understanding how systems can be manipulated, whether through binaries or through sentences. I'm learning every day with all of you. That's why I gave my first ever BSides talk on #offensivesecurity: Polyglot Poisoning - Bypassing AI Guardrails via the Linguistic Supply Chain. I showed how syntactic obfuscation, phonological ambiguity, and code-switching can bypass LLM guardrails that work in English but fail in other languages through a presentation and live demo via Local Ollama with a 80% bypass rate. This research isn't mine alone. It belongs to a community. 🔬 NAMAA Community (Arabic NLP) LLM Security Red Team Mahmoud Reda (CAMel Lab, NYU Abu Dhabi) Abdelbasset Djamai (AI Research Engineer, Datategy) Mohammed Hamdy (Helwan University) You showed me how Egyptian, Levantine, and Saudi Arabic dialectal variation breaks models trained on Modern Standard Arabic. This demo wouldn't exist without our collaboration. 🎓 Guidance & Mentorship Dr. Mona Abdelazim,PhD (Ain Shams University) - your feedback shaped this work. Juli Fowler - For your guidance on public speaking. 📚 Research Community Faruna Godwin Abuh Faruna (LSR Benchmark) Deng et al. (Low-Resource Languages Jailbreak GPT-4) Yong et al. (State of Multilingual LLM Safety) Your papers gave me the foundation to stand on. 🙏 BSides San Diego Thank you to the entire BSides community for welcoming me. This was my first time speaking at your conference and I definitely intend to return. Special thanks to Sofia, Nick, Victor, and everyone who supported me on the way to my presentation. You made this happen. It was also great to meet fellow speaker Janane Suresh who helped me learn more about her topic on SBoms! 👥 Audience To those who stayed after, asked brilliant questions, and affirmed this path of research , you know who you are. Your presence and curiosity keep me going. 🔗 GitHub: https://lnkd.in/giiDR_Wi 📧 Collaborators welcome: thecyberlingo@gmail.com I'm looking for 1-2 collaborators! If you work on multilingual AI safety, want to add your language, or just want to build something better please reach out. Let's continue building together. #PolyglotPoisoning #LinguisticSecurity #BSidesSD #AISafety #MultilingualNLP #NAMAA #OffensiveSecurity #RedTeam #BlueTeam #PurpleTeam #LLMSecurity #AdversarialML #InfoSec #CyberSecurity #AIGovernance #LinguisticSecurityInstitute 52 reactions · 4 comments 52 · 4 comments Post We often speak about AI safety in the abstract, but security is built, or broken, in the details of language. I’m honored to share that I’ll be speaking at BSides San Diego this April on closing the language gap in AI security. In this session, I’ll move from principle to practice: · How linguistic diversity exposes real gaps in model guardrails, not as a flaw, but as a necessary design signal. · Why the data supply chain is the critical layer for defense, and how to harden multilingual pipelines. · Introducing the Linguistic Firewall, a framework for proactive, context-aware security across scripts and dialects. · Releasing a public tool to help teams test and strengthen their own systems. This work is for anyone building, deploying, or securing AI meant for a global audience. Trustworthy systems don't speak one language, they guard them all. Looking forward to sharing more in San Diego! #AISecurity #LLM #Linguistics #GlobalAI #DataSupplyChain #BSidesSD #ResponsibleAI 9 reactions · 1 comment 9 · 1 comment Post Honored to be speaking at the Tech Intersections conference at Northeastern University - Oakland next month. I'll be addressing a critical, systemic gap at the intersection of cybersecurity, AI governance, and linguistics. We design security for systems, not for people. In a multilingual world, that's not an oversight, it's a critical vulnerability. The new reality: In the age of sovereign AI, security is no longer just cryptography. It's cultural synthesis. If your system doesn't understand the language, dialect, and context of its users, it has a security gap. Period. This is why I'm speaking: · For Builders: Your product's resilience depends on its weakest cultural assumption. A model's accuracy is irrelevant if it fails the communities it's meant to serve. · For Leaders: Market trust is now tied to communicating safety in your user's lived terms, not just in your technical jargon. · For Our Future: We either learn to synthesize intelligence from the ground up, with communities, or we face a permanent deficit of security and trust. This is the next competency. This isn't just a talk about a gap. It's a blueprint for the synthesis we need. Join us in Oakland on Jan 24, 2026 if you build, lead, or secure technology meant for the real world. Seats are limited. Secure your ticket at: www.techintersections.org All ticket sales are free. However, if you have professional development dollars, we recommend purchasing a ticket here: https://lnkd.in/e5tRDKAc. Thank you! #Cybersecurity #AI #SovereignAI #TechLeadership #Linguistics #InclusiveDesign #TechIntersections #FutureOfTech #EthicalA 16 reactions · 6 comments 16 · 6 comments Link Portfolio Sahara Al-Madi 🔹 Computational Linguist & AI Ethics Strategist – Building secure, culturally coherent systems where language, technology, and policy intersect. 🔸 Exploring the synthesis of language revitalization, AI security, and ethical governance through iterative projects. 🔗 Portfolio in progress Link Coding for Culture & Connection GitHub 🔹 Coding at the intersection of language revitalization, AI security, and ethical governance. 🔸 Hands-on with Python, JavaScript, and compliance frameworks to build resilient and inclusive systems. 🔹 Committed to technology that preserves cultural nuance, protects community trust, and upholds accountability.

Verified Profile 1st Niaz-Ul Haque, P.Eng. • 1st ☁️ Solution Architect | AWS Cloud | Event-Driven & Modern Architectures | Digital Transformation 4w • 🔐 AI is powerful. But how secure is it? As organizations rapidly adopt AI and Generative AI, one question is becoming increasingly important: How do we protect AI systems themselves? AI models are not just software anymore. They rely on data pipelines, APIs, models, and external integrations. That means the attack surface is larger than traditional applications. Here are a few key areas where cybersecurity must evolve to protect AI systems: • Data Security – AI models are only as trustworthy as the data they are trained on. Protecting training data from poisoning or manipulation is critical. • Model Protection – Prevent unauthorized access to models to avoid model theft, reverse engineering, or misuse. • Secure APIs – Many AI applications expose models through APIs. Strong authentication, rate limiting, and monitoring are essential. • Access Control & Governance – Limit who can interact with models, datasets, and prompts. Proper identity management reduces internal risks. • Monitoring & Threat Detection – AI systems should be continuously monitored for abnormal usage patterns, prompt injections, and suspicious queries. 💡 The reality: AI is transforming industries, but without strong security practices, it can also introduce new risks. The future of technology isn’t just AI-powered. It’s AI-powered and securely built. #CyberSecurity #AIsecurity #Gene

Activity 3,357 followers Posts Comments Videos Images Samuel Adetsi Verified Profile 1st Samuel Adetsi • 1st Machine Learning Engineer & Data Scientist | UBC Master of Data Science | Python · Spark · Kafka · AWS · Production ML Systems 4mo • A few years ago, during my undergraduate days at The University of Cape Coast, I fell in love with IoT. The idea that you could wire sensors and actuators to a microcontroller and bring them to life with just a few lines of code absolutely fascinated me. You’d often find me tinkering with electronics, debugging circuits, or building little systems just to see what I could make them do. Then in 2021, something shifted. One of my IoT projects started generating more data than I had ever worked with. I could see the potential: the insights I could extract and the problems I could solve. But I didn’t yet have the expertise to make sense of it all. That moment sparked my journey into the world of data. I began reading everything I could, experimenting with analytics, and building my first models. But I quickly realized that to truly understand how things work under the hood, I needed structured, deeper training. So I set my sights on formal education in data science. Fast forward to today: I’ve completed my Master of Data Science at The University of British Columbia. The program has given me far more than I imagined: strong foundations, hands-on experience, and the confidence to thrive as a data professional. Now, with my data expertise combined with my software engineering experience, I’m able to design and build efficient, scalable data solutions from the ground up. I’m grateful to everyone who inspired, supported, and believed in me throughout this journey. I’m excited to bring this unique combination of skills to a team here in Canada where I can contribute to solving meaningful, real-world problems with data. #DataScience #DataEngineering #IoT #MachineLearning #Analytics #UBC #TechCareers #CareerGrowth #Graduation2025 #UBCMDS …

Until now, this has remained the case. I've spent the last three months creating something that didn't exist: a ready-to-use, open RAG system and the first unified dataset on tourism in Georgia. The goal of this digital infrastructure is to stimulate tourism in Georgia, not only for the most popular regions, but also for other less popular, but no less beautiful, destinations! And also to preserve its cultural heritage... 1️⃣ To start, I created a custom dataset: You can't build great AI on bad data. Activity 4,998 followers Posts Comments Images Kevin Johnson Premium Profile 1st Kevin Johnson • 1st MSCS @ Northeastern 1mo • Edited • Just shipped a Mentor Finder AI Agent at the Agents of Tomorrow Hackathon Subconscious x ACM at Northeastern University 🤖 The problem: Finding the right mentor is painful. You manually search LinkedIn, view profiles, craft cold DMs hoping you find the right person to seek guidance from. What we built in 3 hours: → Resume PDF → Python pipeline extracts & structures your skills, title, domain & industry into a clean JSON profile → Structured JSON feeds directly into an AI agent powered by TIM-GPT on Subconscious.dev → Agent orchestrates 6 tools (People Search, Web Search, Google Search, Company Search) in real-time → Returns publicly available 10 senior professionals matching your profile with LinkedIn, email, portfolio & why they’re a good mentor fit What I loved about this technically: ● The resume parser is a mini data pipeline PDF ingestion → text extraction → schema-driven JSON output ● TIM-GPT handles the agentic reasoning decomposing the task, running parallel searches, synthesizing results ● The agent layer is pure agentic AI multi-tool orchestration, real-time web retrieval, prompt-driven reasoning ● Together they form a clean DE + AI stack: structured data in, intelligent output out Built with: Python · Streamlit · pdfplumber · Subconscious.dev . Featured Post I'm delighted to share that our paper was accepted to the 2024 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech & Signal Processing (ICASSP)! Title: Leveraging Data Collection And Unsupervised Learning For Code-Switched Tunisian Arabic Automatic Speech Recognition This paper addresses the challenge of Automatic Speech Recognition (ASR) for the Tunisian dialect, introduces new resources for code-switched Tunisian Arabic defining a very challenging ASR task in spontaneous audio involving three languages: Arabic, French, and English. The paper also proposes a solid baseline model for Tunisian code-switched ASR. Link: https://lnkd.in/dtYKXXeg #ICASSP2024 My paper was accepted to the 2024 IEEE International Conference on Acoustics, Speech & Signal Processing (ICASSP). Join me! invt.io 40 reactions · 7 comments 40 · 7 comments Post Hello Everyone, Over the past few months, we’ve been working on developing an Automatic Speech Recognition model that can handle both the Tunisian Dialect and the code-switching in the Tunisian speech. To overcome the challenge of limited Tunisian dialect data, we worked hard to collect and carefully label audio and text from different situations, including natural conversations and formal readings, as well as when people switch between languages and when they speak only Tunisian. All the data we collected is now available and accessible at this link: https://lnkd.in/dbE2BuME Moreover, our trained models are readily accessible on Huggingface. We have also established two demo spaces featuring our best models, one with code-switching and one without, both of which are fully operational and accessible here: https://lnkd.in/dU9tjFSy I'd like to give a heartfelt thank you to my supervisor, Salah Zaiem, for providing invaluable guidance and unwavering support throughout this remarkable journey. top_sections: Régis N'guessan · 1st PhD Student in Applied Mathematics and Computational Science, AIMS RIC || Non-Archimedean Mathematics || Speech and Language Processing || Machine Reasoning || AI || Embedding & Intelligent systems Kigali City, Rwanda · Contact info African Institute for Mathematical Sciences (AIMS) 8,666 followers · 500+ connections Olga, Ashwin and 2 other mutual connections Message More

About I’m a PhD student in Applied Mathematics and Computational Sciences, with a deep interest in how rigorous mathematics can drive meaningful advances in AI. My PhD research focuses on developing robust neural architectures for Text-to-Speech systems in low-resource languages, combining optimization theory, computational modeling, and deep learning. Beyond this, I’m particularly drawn to non-Archimedean mathematics, exploring p-adic and ultrametric structures, and how these ideas can inspire new AI architectures and reasoning frameworks. I’ve contributed to projects across mathematics and engineering, and I’m also building novel frameworks in machine reasoning and computational consciousness, pushing toward foundational insights in AGI. … more

Featured Post 🚨 New Preprint Alert I’m excited to share my latest preprint, “Müntz-Szász Networks: Neural Architectures with Learnable Power-Law Bases,” now available on arXiv. Standard architectures (MLPs) rely on fixed activation functions such as ReLU or tanh. While they are universal approximators, they are highly inefficient at modeling functions with singular or fractional power behavior-structures that arise naturally in physics, including fracture mechanics, boundary layers, and corner singularities. The idea: Instead of imposing fixed activation functions, Müntz-Szász Networks (MSN) treat the functional form itself as learnable. The network learns the exponents of power-law functions directly from data, alongside the weights. The architecture is grounded in the classical Müntz-Szász theorem from approximation theory, which characterizes when power-law bases form a dense and expressive representation. Architecturally, the nonlinearity lives on the edges rather than at the nodes, with each edge computing a learnable power-law map instead of a fixed activation. Key findings: 🚀 Efficiency: MSN achieves 5-8× lower error than standard MLPs using up to 10× fewer parameters on singular regression tasks. ⚛️ AI for science: On physics-informed neural networks (PINNs), including singular ODEs, MSN yields 3-6× improvements. 🔍 Interpretability: The learned exponents reflect the underlying structure of the solution. For a √x target, the network converges to exponents near 0.5. I’d be very happy to hear feedback, questions, or related perspectives from people working on scientific ML, PINNs, or approximation theory. The full paper is available here: 👉 https://lnkd.in/dk8a5UDN Acknowledgement: AIMS Research and Innovation Centre , Axiom Research Group. #DeepLearning #ScientificML #AppliedMathematics #AI4Science #NeuralNetworks #PhysicsInformedNN #ApproximationTheory #MachineLearningResearch 117 reactions · 20 comments 117 · 20 comments Post 🚨 New Preprint Alert Thrill

The full paper is available here: 👉 https://lnkd.in/dk8a5UDN Acknowledgement: AIMS Research and Innovation Centre , Axiom Research Group. #DeepLearning #ScientificML #AppliedMathematics #AI4Science #NeuralNetworks #PhysicsInformedNN #ApproximationTheory #MachineLearningResearch 117 reactions · 20 comments 117 · 20 comments Post 🚨 New Preprint Alert Thrilled to share my latest research is now on arXiv! 🚀 https://lnkd.in/eBdjJhmN After years of work bridging number theory and deep learning, I introduce van der Put Neural Networks (v-PuNNs), to our knowledge the first class of scalable neural architectures designed to operate natively in p-adic ultrametric space. This work addresses a long-standing geometry mismatch: Traditional deep learning models embed hierarchical data (WordNet, Gene Ontology, taxonomies) in Euclidean space(ℝᵈ), which fails to reflect their tree-like structure and introduces geometric distortion. Mathematicians have long suspected the natural setting is p-adic ultrametric space (ℚₚ or ℤₚ), where distances are determined by shared ancestry rather than linear position. What’s new in v-PuNNs? 🔬 Van der Put basis: First use of this classical p-adic basis (1968) in deep learning. Each neuron corresponds to a p-adic ball (a subtree), yielding exact interpretability and a rigorous mathematical grounding. The name v-PuNNs honors Marius van der Put. 📐 Finite Hierarchical Approximation Theorem: Proves any function on a K-level hierarchy can be represented exactly with ∑ pʲ neurons, bridging infinite p-adic expansions with finite data and ensuring minimal parameters for universal expressiveness. 🌳 Transparent Ultrametric Representation Learning (TURL): Parameters are p-adic numbers encoding explicit ancestor paths, making representations fully white-box and directly interpretable. ⚡ Valuation-Adaptive Perturbation Optimization (VAPO): Derivative-free optimizer tailored to ultrametric geometry, with two modes: GIST-VAPO (fast search) and Adam-VAPO (moment-based). 🌀 Geometric characterization: Fractal geometry and entropy analyses reveal the self-similar nature of learned hierarchies and quantify conceptual complexity across depth. Our practical implementation HiPaN trains 52k-leaf WordNet in ~16 minutes on CPU-only hardware, with strict ultrametric fidelity and full interpretability. It outperforms MLPs, hierarchical softmax, XGBoost, and LightGBM on WordNet nouns (NLP/lexical semantics), Gene Ontology molecular function (bioinformatics), NCBI Mammalia taxonomy (biology/taxonomy). Variants like HiPaQ (quantum) and Tab-HiPaN (tabular) broaden applications to other domains. Acknowledgement: AIMS Research and Innovation Centre and @ΣηIGmα Research Group #Mathematics #AI #DeepLearning #NumberTheory #MachineLearning #pAdic #Ultrametric #InterpretableAI #Research #ArtificialIntelligence #NLP #Bioinformatics #KnowledgeGraphs #QuantumAI #Geometry #Topology #HierarchicalLearning #Arxiv #AIResearch #CPUOnlyAI #EfficientAI 138 reactions · 42 comments 138 · 42 comments Post 🚨 Preprint Out | A New Primitive for Artificial Consciousness 📄 The Reflexive Integrated Information Unit (RIIU) I’m proud to share that the first preprint in our series of work that aims to bring mathematical clarity and modular design to one of the most elusive problems in AI: machine consciousness is now available on arxiv. In this paper, we introduce the Reflexive Integrated Information Unit (RIIU), a differentiable recurrent module that combines information integration, reflexive self-modeling, and global broadcasting. It’s designed to be(at the end) to consciousness research what the perceptron was to pattern recognition: a small, trainable unit you can plug into systems, benchmark, and evolve. ⚠️ But let’s be clear: This is not consciousness yet, and we don’t claim it is. It’s a computational substrate that instantiates three conditions widely cited by major theories of consciousness (IIT, Global Workspace, Higher-Order Thought). Whether those are sufficient for phenomenology is well beyond our scope. 🎯 What the RIIU does offer: A minimal, differentiable unit for studying integration and self-reference A local, trainable surrogate for Φ (Auto-Φ), computed efficiently Three formal results: compositionality, end-to-end differentiability, and Φ-monotone plasticity A PyTorch implementation and diagnostic experiments in an agent with actuator damage 🧪 In tests, a four-layer RIIU agent recovers twice as fast as a GRU-based baseline after failure, while preserving a non-zero Auto-Φ signal. It's not "aware," but it does track and react to its own computational footprint. 📐 Mathematically, this is a step toward treating consciousness-like properties with the same rigor we apply to learning theory and optimization. We define the RIIU in precise terms, derive its differentiability, and prove theoretical guarantees about how it scales and behaves under gradient ascent. 🔭 Next steps: This is just the beginning. Future papers in the series will expand the architecture, extend to embodied settings, explore deeper integration metrics, and test behavior on complex cognitive tasks. RIIU is a starting point, not a conclusion. 👉 Find the Preprint arXiv: https://lnkd.in/gzEQ2Xvu All code, proofs, and ablation studies are open: 👉 https://lnkd.in/gVhKyhYT Huge thanks to my co-author Issa Karambal, Ph.D. , the ΣηIGmα Research Group , the AIMS Research and Innovation Centre .We hope this work helps move artificial consciousness from speculative architectures to concrete, empirical mathematics. #artificialconsciousness #deeplearning #machinereasoning #reinforcementlearning #consciousAI #arxiv #mathematics #causalinference #modularAI #AIresearch 68 reactions · 20 comments 68 · 20 comments Reshared by Régis N'guessan New Preprint Alert: Proud to share our latest work with the ΣηIGmα Research Group, now available as a preprint on arXiv! 📄 Localized Weather Prediction Using Kolmogorov-Arnold Network-Based Models and Deep RNNs Find the paper: https://lnkd.in/etZQKs8H In this paper, we explored how to bring mathematical elegance and interpretability into real-world weather forecasting using Kolmogorov-Arnold Networks (KANs) and Temporal KANs (TKANs). These architectures are relatively new and offer a compelling alternative to traditional RNNs for modeling complex, localized, and nonlinear climate patterns, especially in data-scarce regions like tropical Africa. 🔍 Using real-world data from Abidjan and Kigali, we benchmarked models for daily temperature, precipitation, and pressure forecasting. KANs showed remarkable performance in temperature prediction (R² > 0.99), and our TKAN variants improved precipitation forecasts, especially in low-rainfall regimes. 🔧 All experiments were implemented in PyTorch and are available here: https://lnkd.in/e_X7H4KP 🙏 Special thanks to Liu et al. for the original KAN architecture and to Rémi Genet and Hugo Inzirillo for their contribution on TKANs. It’s exciting to build on such foundational work. And also to AIMS Research and Innovation Centre. 👥 Co-authored with Ange-Clément Akazan, Verlon Roel MBINGUI, and Issa Karambal, Ph.D. This work is a small step toward more interpretable, adaptive, and robust AI for climate resilience. #KAN #TKAN #MathInAI #WeatherForecasting #DeepLearning #AI4Africa #ClimateScience #SplineNetworks #TimeSeries #AIMS #arXiv #ΣηIGmαResearchGroup 21 reactions · 2 comments 21 · 2 comments Post 📚 Découvrez mon premier livre "APPRENDRE LA PROGRAMMATION PYTHON EN RESOLVANT DES PROBLÈMES AFRICAINS" 🌍🐍 🎉 Hello à tous, étudiants , enseignants, élèves, developpeurs, professionnels, personne en quête de reconversion/réorientation professionnelle, passionnés de technologie et amis de l'apprentissage ! profiles.about: About I’m a PhD student in Applied Mathematics and Computational Sciences, with a deep interest in how rigorous mathematics can drive meaningful advances in AI. My PhD research focuses on developing robust neural architectures for Text-to-Speech systems in low-resource languages, combining optimization theory, computational modeling, and deep learning. Beyond this, I’m particularly drawn to non-Archimedean mathematics, exploring p-adic and ultrametric structures, and how these ideas can inspire new AI architectures and reasoning frameworks. I’ve contributed to projects across mathematics and engineering, and I’m also building novel frameworks in machine reasoning and computational consciousness, pushing toward foundational insights in AGI. … more

section_map.Featured: Featured Post I climbed that mountain in my LinkedIn profile banner - last month. It's the 8th highest mountain in the world. 3 years ago I had another mountain in my profile ... and I climbed it, a year after I replaced the other mountain I had on my banner, because I'd climbed it the year before that. I never knew how or when I was going to climb any of them ... until I pasted them into my profile, and imprinted them on my brain and told myself to "figure it out". Then year after year I would view every decision I made whether family, love, career or money through the lens of that objective. When I look back on the last 10 years I can't believe what I've accomplished in the mountains. I've impressed even ME, and I'm my harshest critic by a longshot. And those other things I 'sacrificed' in pursuit of those singular personal (selfish) objectives ... well, ask my son, my friends, my family, and the players I coach if they think they got anything less than 100% of Justin every time we were together. Work on YOU every day, set unimaginable personal objectives ... you'll know you're on the right track when you find yourself thinking "that's impossible!" ... and when you hear that ... WRITE THAT IDEA DOWN. GIVE IT OXYGEN. TELL OTHER PEOPLE. MAKE IT REAL. You'll be amazed at what happens next! And me ... well I'm currently dreaming the next impossible dream. Stay tuned for a change in my profile picture? 102 reactions · 9 comments 102 · 9 comments Post Let's remember to take a minute ... and cry. .... I crested this never-ending pitch on our summit push not knowing how much longer we had until we reached the summit ... it's sunrise, I'm in the Himalayas, my watch tells me I'm at 8,111m in elevation and my Sherpa and I are the highest humans on the planet in that moment ... and in that moment, I paused to catch my breath after 5 hours of steady ascent without more than 20 minutes rest, and as I looked up I'm instantly struck ... there in front of me is the final summit ridge of Manaslu, the exact image I'd been staring at on my phone for motivation over the last 2 years. It's a moment and a feeling I'll never forget ... I stop and hit Pasang my Sherpa and through my oxygen mask I blurt-out "that's the summit ridge, we did it" he replies hurriedly "I know I know ... we have to go. More climbers coming, single track on last ridge ... summit summit, let's go!" I turn my gaze away from that ridge for a brief moment and calmly tell him that I'd been staring at this image for 2 years, over 500 workouts of at least 1,000 hours, and that we'd be fine for this one minute ... he saw the look of sheer joy on my face as I spoke those words through my tears, and he softened ... and then he agreed ... and I cried for every second of that minute as he put his arm around me to share the moment ... we're not even on the summit yet! We're simply standing in front of the final pitch, but we both know the summit is an inevitability ... and in many ways this penultimate moment was more emotional for me than standing on the true summit 20 minutes later ... I'm grateful I knew to lean into this moment even though it might seem premature ... I climb for the epic achievements that I tell maybe 50 people about, but I mostly climb for these emotions, to feel these feelings, that only 1 person knows ... ME What lessons from these experiences do I share with my son, my colleagues, and my players ... I tell them to cry on top of mountains, whatever their 'mountain' may be. I tell them to embrace every win, to always acknowledge when hard work delivers a breakthrough no matter how big or small, to never forget that everything in life requires a team ... and to always remember to take that minute! 73 reactions · 13 comments 73 · 13 comments Post Leaving my son for 4 weeks and risking my life to climb a mountain is MY oxygen. I'm a better father the other 48 weeks because of it! What do you NEED to do that seems selfish, and ego-centric but actually makes you FAR BETTER for everyone else in your life afterward? #millennials #goodluckandgoodselling #contenderschampionsandyou #leadership #saassales #millennialgeneration #b2bsaas #leadershipdevelopment 32 reactions · 18 comments 32 · 18 comments Post Teaching on my brain this week as my son begins his university career! Did I teach him enough over these last 17 years? I don't think I did ... and I wish it were different. I can't fix that mistake ... but I won't repeat the mistake over the next 17 years. What will YOU do next? #millennials #goodluckandgoodselling #contenderschampionsandyou #leadership #saassales #millennialgeneration #b2bsaas 16 reactions · 2 comments 16 · 2 comments Post HUG A MODEL TODAY! profiles.about: About Enterprise B2B SaaS CRO and VP Sales with a documented track record of repeatedly growing $0–$30M ARR companies and leading successful exits for early to mid-stage enterprise vendors selling into Global 2000 companies. Known for building scalable revenue engines and leading high-performing sales teams focused on repeatable growth through disciplined adherence to sales process and sales methodologies, including MEDDPICC, Sandler, Voss, and JOLT. And let's not forget saas metrics, the go-to anchor for data-led decision making. At Totogi, we grew the customer base from 2 to 8 within the first 15 months and achieved a deal win-rate of 39%. Built a robust sales pipeline with a TOFU-to-BOFU closed-won conversion rate of 11%, while coaching the sales team to competitive advantage through hands-on training and consistent reinforcement of our core sales languages - the 4 methodologies. At Risk Control Technologies, led a 3-year 19% CAGR resulting in an acquisition by the #2 global InsurTech software company, while dramatically improving key unit economics, including CAC, CAC payback, LTV:CAC, and Magic Number. Built a repeatable demand generation engine delivering triple the monthly pipeline compared to pre-2021 levels. Earlier at RightWorks, grew revenue from $5M to $33M ARR in 20 months and closed the largest deal in company history at $7.5M ACV. Expertise in Enterprise B2B SaaS sales across Insurance (Tier 1–4 carriers and brokers), Financial Services, Telecom (global MNO and MVNO), Government, and Professional Services markets throughout North America and all global markets. Skilled in driving and leading teams to drive complex, multi-stakeholder, multi-year ACV deals with a mix of direct and partner sales motions, including BDR teams, reseller channels, strategic alliances, system integrators, ABM/ABX programs, and conference- and LinkedIn-led demand generation. - Revenue leadership: GTM strategy, ICP definition, sales process design, hands-on enterprise deal coaching, forecast accuracy, board reporting - Sales methodologies: MEDDPICC, Sandler, Voss, JOLT ... to de-risk scale and repeatability - Demand generation: ABM/ABX, outbound prospecting, SDR/BDR teams, partner channels, strategic alliances, AI BDR/SDR solutions - SaaS Metrics and RevOps: pipeline conversion rates, cost/MQL and cost/SQL analysis, win-rate, CAC, payback period, LTV:CAC ratio, Magic Number, ACV expansion, logo retention, NRR - Markets: Insurance, Property & Casualty, Commercial Lines, Financial Services, Telecom MNO and MVNO, MarTech, InsurTech … more Top skills Enterprise Software Sales • Software as a Service (SaaS) • Go-to-Market Strategy • Sales Leadership • revenue operations

profiles.title: Anna Kiswani | LinkedIn
top_sections: Anna Kiswani She/Her · 1st Build and deploy AI employees that run like software on Oya.ai United States · Contact info Message More

About Ten years of product management taught me one thing above everything else: most of the work that slows companies down shouldn't require a human at all. I'm AI Native Product Manager and co-founder of Oya.ai. I help businesses deploy autonomous AI workers that handle the operations, outreach, and reporting that used to eat 30+ hours a week across their best people. Unlike chatbots or automation tools, the AI employees I help deploy are compiled to deterministic code - same quality output, every single time, fully auditable, at a fraction of the cost of software or headcount. My background spans 10+ years of PM work across SaaS, platforms, APIs, and growth. I bring all of that to making AI deployment feel less like a science experiment and more like a business decision. If you're a founder, operator, or exec wondering how to actually put AI to work in your company - not just experiment with it - I'd love to connect. … more

Featured Post Stop treating AI agents like senior engineers. They aren't. They’re interns with photographic memories but zero common sense. The biggest mistake I see teams making right now? Trying to 'prompt' their way into reliability. If your AI employee relies on a 2,000-word prompt to not break your database, you don't have a production system. You have a ticking clock. At Oya, we’ve shifted the philosophy: Don't just prompt. Compile. We treat AI logic like software code—version-controlled, isolated in secure sandboxes, and compiled into executable Python. Because 'vibes-based engineering' doesn't scale to a $100M pipeline. If you’re moving an agent from a demo to a department, you need more than a better model. You need a runtime that enforces boundaries and catches errors before they hit your customer data. Are you still using 'mega-prompts,' or have you started building actual guardrails? #AIAgents #SoftwareEngineering #OyaAI 12, number of reactions 12 3 comments
