About I am a Machine Learning and Applied AI engineer with hands-on experience building systems across LLMs, RAG, NLP, computer vision, and backend infrastructure. I enjoy turning AI ideas into practical systems that are reliable, measurable, and deployable. At the University at Buffalo, I have built real-time AI applications including edge-deployed computer vision systems, LLM-powered control pipelines, and retrieval-grounded medical QA assistants. My work has included model fine-tuning, evaluation harnesses, FastAPI services, FAISS-based retrieval, and safety-focused post-training workflows. Before that, I worked as a Software Engineer at HotWax Systems, where I built data-validation and workflow reliability systems that reduced failures and improved operational efficiency. That mix of software engineering and AI experience has shaped how I approach building production-ready ML systems. I am currently looking for full-time roles as a Machine Learning Engineer, Applied AI Engineer, or AI Engineer. … more Top skills Machine Learning • Large Language Models (LLM) • Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) • Natural Language Processing (NLP) • Python (Programming Language)

section_map.Featured: Featured Post 🎉 Excited to announce that Creator Finder is now an open source app! Have you ever discovered a creator/influencer on YouTube and wanted to support them elsewhere, but couldn't find their page? Or opened an account on a social media and don't see a familiar face to follow? That's exactly why I built Creator Finder. It's a web app that helps you: — Automatically find where your favorite creators post content across different platforms — Import your YouTube subscriptions with just one click — Discover and support creators through their preferred channels — Find who to follow on Patreon, Twitter, Instagram, Discord … — Keep your data private (private data stays on your device) What started as a personal project to solve a common frustration has grown into a full-featured platform that makes creator discovery seamless across platforms. I've documented the full technical journey and implementation details in my projects section. Feel free to check it out to learn more about the architecture and development process! 🔗 Try it out: https://lnkd.in/gGgNVMx5 📦 Source code: https://lnkd.in/gtKV-KY2 #OpenSource #CreatorEconomy #WebDevelopment #EdgeComputing #Cloudflare #Influencer 28 reactions · 3 comments 28 · 3 comments Post An #AI creating images from text is what we need more in the earth! Have you ever dreamed a cat alligator chimera? Thanks Janelle Shane for blogging about this. Thanks OpenAI for teaching DALL·E about life. https://lnkd.in/eZEtmzP via https://lnkd.in/eEhy8QT #artificialintelligence #deeplearning 3 reactions · 1 comment 3 · 1 comment

About I focus on ambiguous, high-impact problem spaces — defining technical direction, shaping long-term platform strategy, and building durable systems and mechanisms that scale teams. I spend much of my time unblocking cross-org initiatives, mentoring senior engineers, and improving how organizations make and execute technical decisions. A growing part of my work sits at the intersection of AI-enabled builders and engineering excellence. AI is expanding who gets to build software — from engineers to product leaders and executives. I work on the technical and organizational problems that arise when creation becomes easy, but operating systems at scale still require rigor. My focus is on translating rapid experimentation into durable, reliable platforms through architecture, hands-on development, and enabling processes. … more Top skills Amazon Web Services (AWS) • Artificial Intelligence (AI) • Team Leadership • Cloud Computing

Featured Post After completing 14 certified courses, I'm pleased to share that I've earned the IBM DevOps and Software Engineering Professional Certificate from Coursera. For detailed course content, please refer to the certificate below. My favorite moments during this program were the hands-on projects, particularly: - Creating and deploying an AI-based application on a web server using IBM Watson AI Libraries and Flask [Python] (source code: https://lnkd.in/egrisHap) - The DevOps Capstone Project (https://lnkd.in/e9xUFqGf) These projects enhanced my understanding of the DevOps cycle and CRUD methodologies in the Software Development life cycle. During the Capstone project, I applied the concepts from the courses to build a Customer Accounts microservice. I developed an Agile plan, managed the project using GitHub and Zenhub, created a product backlog, and executed a sprint plan. I used test-driven development (TDD), GitHub Actions, and secure code practices, and automated deployment to Kubernetes using a Tekton pipeline. For more details, please refer to my Capstone project repository on GitHub: https://lnkd.in/e9xUFqGf. Completing these projects and obtaining this certification has fueled my passion to continue learning about DevOps and Software Engineering. I look forward to sharing more related milestones and projects with you here on LinkedIn. Good evening! IBM DevOps and Software Engineering 176, number of reactions 176 26 comments · 1 repost

Featured Post Had a pleasure to do a podcast with Leanne Alessandra Bevan from Grey Matter Discussed Agentic AI Development, Jetbrains and of course Junie 51 reactions · 4 comments 51 · 4 comments Post I am happy to share that Junie CLI by JetBrains is live 🚀 Install with one line https://lnkd.in/dCwCbByj Junie CLI brings access to our agent outside of JetBrains IDEs. It is available directly from the terminal (Mac, Win, or Linux), inside any IDE, and in CI/CD (GitHub or GitLab). Junie allows setup or one-click migration of your skills, guidelines, and MCPs from other agents such as Claude Code, Codex, and others. Junie CLI also allows you to Bring Your Own Key (BYOK) and run the agent without a JetBrains AI license. 171 reactions · 10 comments 171 · 10 comments Post Full house at my presentation at DEVWorld Conference. Activity 1,004 followers Posts Comments Images Articles Documents Dmitry Degtyarev Verified Profile 1st Dmitry Degtyarev • 1st AWS Cloud Cost | Optimization | Migration | Architecture | Development 1mo • Edited • One reason humans still have an edge over AI isn’t “creativity”. It’s retraining speed. Most LLMs don’t actually learn while they’re helping you. During inference, the model’s parameters stay fixed; the only “memory” it gets is what you put into the prompt. In other words: weights are static, context is temporary. We keep trying to solve that by shoving more into the context window: 128k… 1M… more. But max context ≠ usable context. Research is pretty blunt about long-context reliability: Models often show a U-shaped behavior — they use information best at the beginning and end, and struggle when the key fact is buried in the middle ( google “lost in the middle”). Even when you control for retrieval (i.e., the model can access the evidence), simply making the input longer can sharply degrade task performance — sometimes well below the advertised window size. That lines up with what I see at work: A human who spends a week or two inside an org can internalize the real operating system — decision history, exceptions, the “why”, ownership boundaries, and the unwritten rules — in a way that’s hard to replicate by pasting 200 pages of docs into a prompt. So here’s my near‑term bet: Don’t fine-tune your brain on what the internet already taught the models. If your plan is to spend months memorizing React components, AWS service trivia, or Python syntax… that’s low ROI. The models already have that in their weights, and they’ll keep getting better. Instead, fine-tune on proprietary context: - internal policies and the exceptions people actually apply - how decisions get made (and who really influences them) - system constraints + incident history + “things we never do again” - roles, ownership, dependencies, and relationships That’s still a human advantage: you can

Featured Media The Skewness and Kurtosis of European Options and the Implications for Trade Sizing In this note, we calculate the skewness and kurtosis for European calls and puts. Next, using a power series expansion, we derive a simple approximation to the Kelly fraction for optimal trade sizing when returns are non-normal. Taken together, these two results are used to show that the higher order moments of option positions have an economically significant effect on optimal trade sizing when using the Kelly criterion.

section_map.Publications: Publications The Skewness and Kurtosis of European Options and the Implications for Trade Sizing SSRN · Apr 21, 2017 Show publication Long vanilla call option positions have unlimited profit potential and limited loss potential, and the opposite is true for short call option positions. Long vanilla puts do not have unlimited profit potential but, for commonly traded strikes, the maximum profit of a long put will be far greater than the maximum loss (the premium of the option). Again, the opposite is true for short put positions: their maximum loss is far greater than the potential gain. Equivalently, option returns are highly skewed and kurtotic. In this note, we calculate the skewness and kurtosis for European calls and puts. Next, using a power series expansion, we derive a simple approximation to the Kelly fraction for optimal trade sizing when returns are non-normal. Taken together, these two results are used to show that the higher order moments of option positions have an economically significant effect on optimal trade sizing when using the Kelly criterion. … more

About I turn advanced computer vision and AI technologies into operational systems that scale. From building a computer vision lab in a barn to deploying AI that works as advertised to thousands of live cameras, I’ve led every step of the process: model design, data pipelines, product roadmap, and post-deployment optimization. I’ve hired and led teams across continents, collaborated with defense, education, and retail clients, and pushed innovation through technical, operational, and regulatory constraints My approach is deeply operational: tight feedback loops, custom data tooling, automated validation, and models that ship. I work at the intersection of technical systems, product strategy, and measurable impact. Feel free to connect with me via LinkedIn, 785-219-9212, or marcusday3586@gmail.com to discuss opportunities, collaborations, or innovations in AI and technology that can drive meaningful change. … more Top skills Artificial Intelligence (AI) • Data Modeling • Product Development • Machine Learning • Product Innovation

From my wife, Haiyan, who will be a multi gold medalist in the Annotation Olympics someday when that exists, to Quinn Graehling, who has already forgotten more about machine learning than I'll ever hope to understand. From Emily Sulzer, who has curated the best weapon detection training dataset and manages the happiest annotation team on Earth until proven otherwise, to Samuel Mohebban, who has created IP from scratch that would make you blush if you only knew. Special thanks to Kahle Loveless and Keith Badger who built our first AI Lab with me in the barn. I'm incredibly fortunate to be a part of the ZeroEyes team. You could be, too. Email me marcus@zeroeyes.com if you believe in our mission and think you could contribute to it. section_map.Experience: Experience AI Operations Trilogy · Full-time Jan 2026 - Present · 4 mos Fort Smith, Arkansas, United States · Remote Advisory Board Member / Consultant Syght · Part-time 2024 - Present · 2 yrs 4 mos Boulder, Colorado, United States · Remote New Venture Development, Business Strategy and +1 skill Executive Vice President of Research & Development ZeroEyes · Full-time 2019 - 2024 · 5 yrs Conshohocken, Pennsylvania, United States · Hybrid Led the AI and research division, driving the development and deployment of advanced weapon detection technology. Scaled the company from a startup to an industry leader with 250+ clients and over 35,000 active cameras deployed. Focused on innovation, operational efficiency, and cross-functional collaboration to position the organization as the leader in the field. Built the first ZeroEyes AI lab in a Kansas barn for under $5,000 in 2021, generating a continuous stream of diverse, high-quality data. Achieved a 22x improvement in recall and a 3x increase in precision, enabling detection in nearly any environment. Led the development of quantifiable competitive advantages that secured over $10M in legislative funding and $11M in Department of Defense contracts. Achieved 100% on-time delivery of all contract line items through cost-effective solutions, ensuring client satisfaction and exceptional project outcomes. Played a key role in Series A, Bridge, and Series B funding rounds by documenting and presenting ZeroEyes AI capabilities to support due diligence and investor communication. Invented proprietary AI tools (ZEAT, ZAFT, ZOOT, and ZNT) to enhance performance and optimize neural network training, leading the development and implementation from a product management perspective. Developed advanced data management systems to streamline model training, testing, and evaluation processes. Conducted a bias analysis for a Fortune 10 client, improving detection accuracy. Built and led a diverse, high-performing team, creating 50+ jobs and fostering career growth through leadership development and promotions. Unified the team under the shared mission to "detect any gun on any camera," emphasizing the impact of their work. Led budgeting, forecasting, and variance analysis processes, collaborating with cross-functional teams to meet organizational goals. Built a comprehensive model to project facilities and personnel costs for strategic scenarios, including relocating company headquarters to a lower-cost location and shifting tech hiring to Europe. These insights directly informed the company’s long-term operational strategy. Designed a long-term headcount planning model to guide strategic tech hiring initiatives, aligning workforce planning with organizational growth objectives. Enhanced quarterly cash flow forecast accuracy by 500%, significantly improving financial visibility and decision-making. Conducted weekly P&L Flash meetings with the CFO, presenting actionable updates on progress toward budget objectives and facilitating informed decision-making. About Hey, I'm Donald. I'm interested in a lot of different things. I wish I could pause my life for ten years and go study them all. Right now, I'm a software developer with a background in finance and literature. I enjoy building things, primarily web applications that concern themselves with usefulness. As I build, I seek out new, fun, unlikely ways to abstract my code. Likewise, I get a kick out of constraints and the creativity required to reify solutions around them. Learning is fun! I also have a lot of experience in client relations; alongside my technical know-how, this makes me pretty versatile and well-rounded. Have an idea you're curious to discuss? Feel free to reach out. I've recently been interested in: ai + infra, generative ui, agent harnesses … more

Highlights You both worked at Gauntlet AI Yavuz worked at Gauntlet AI before you started Message

About Better at learning science than most engineers. Better at engineering than most learning scientists. I build two things. First: a data integration platform that connects 20+ learning apps that don't offer APIs. Extracting, normalizing, and unifying student activity data across an entire school network. Think Plaid, but for education. Second: an AI-powered browser agent that navigates ed-tech apps autonomously and evaluates whether they actually teach. It finds where students can shortcut around the learning. Most ed-tech optimizes for completion rates while nobody checks if anyone retained anything. The Learning Theater. Python, TypeScript, Temporal, Playwright, Claude API, Next.js, AWS, PostgreSQL. If you're building AI agent systems or care whether ed-tech produces real learning, message me. … more Top skills AI Agents • Artificial Intelligence (AI) • Large Language Models (LLM) • Data Engineering • TypeScript

profiles.title: Achent Chawla | LinkedIn
top_sections: Achent Chawla · 1st Founding BDM | Early-Stage GTM | Enterprise AI & DevOps Greater London, England, United Kingdom · Contact info Cosine University of Nottingham Message More

About Founding BDM at Cosine, building go-to-market around AI systems for engineering teams. Operating at the intersection of technical product and commercial strategy, translating complex capability into clear business value and sustainable revenue.

Featured Post This morning, we hosted a breakfast panel with OpenAI at Shoreditch House. One month into Cosine and already sitting in a room with leaders from the UK Home Office, Babcock International Group and the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology debating how to actually deploy AI coding tools when the stakes are real. One thing that really stood out: Banks and Defence want AI coding, but they have the highest barrier to entry compared to any other sector. Data sovereignty, air-gapped environments, auditability. Most AI tooling doesn't even make it to evaluation. That gap between demand and deployability is exactly why I joined Cosine. We're enabling AI coding in the most scrutinised and difficult environments. Built for Banks and Defence. Huge thank you to the brilliant Rachel Higham for chairing, and to our panel speakers Rob Thompson, Alice Guo, Dr. Peter Passaro, Lizzie Greenhalgh who joined our CEO, Alistair Pullen. Really enjoyed connecting with everyone who attended today. Great weather and great people! 103 reactions · 16 comments 103 · 16 comments Post After two formative years at VoxSmart, I’ve joined Cosine as a Founding BDM. I joined VoxSmart through qualifying onto the Jumpstart programme as an SDR and did not expect how quickly things would move. I was trusted to lead and scale the entire BD function, building enterprise pipeline in regulated markets and working closely with compliance leaders across global financial institutions. That level of responsibility early on has shaped me more than I anticipated. I’m especially grateful to Oliver Blower for backing my progression and trusting me with real ownership from day one. I was also fortunate to work alongside such an exceptional team who set high standards and pushed me to grow quickly. VoxSmart gave me exposure to complex enterprise environments, demanding buyers, and high-bar execution. I’ll be carrying all these earned lessons forward in my new chapter. 131 reactions · 53 comments

section_map.About: About Founding BDM at Cosine, building go-to-market around AI systems for engineering teams. Operating at the intersection of technical product and commercial strategy, translating complex capability into clear business value and sustainable revenue.
section_map.Featured: Featured Post This morning, we hosted a breakfast panel with OpenAI at Shoreditch House. One month into Cosine and already sitting in a room with leaders from the UK Home Office, Babcock International Group and the Department for Science, Innovation and Technology debating how to actually deploy AI coding tools when the stakes are real. One thing that really stood out: Banks and Defence want AI coding, but they have the highest barrier to entry compared to any other sector. Data sovereignty, air-gapped environments, auditability. Most AI tooling doesn't even make it to evaluation. That gap between demand and deployability is exactly why I joined Cosine. We're enabling AI coding in the most scrutinised and difficult environments. Built for Banks and Defence. Huge thank you to the brilliant Rachel Higham for chairing, and to our panel speakers Rob Thompson, Alice Guo, Dr. Peter Passaro, Lizzie Greenhalgh who joined our CEO, Alistair Pullen. Really enjoyed connecting with everyone who attended today. Great weather and great people! 103 reactions · 16 comments 103 · 16 comments Post After two formative years at VoxSmart, I’ve joined Cosine as a Founding BDM. I joined VoxSmart through qualifying onto the Jumpstart programme as an SDR and did not expect how quickly things would move. I was trusted to lead and scale the entire BD function, building enterprise pipeline in regulated markets and working closely with compliance leaders across global financial institutions. That level of responsibility early on has shaped me more than I anticipated. I’m especially grateful to Oliver Blower for backing my progression and trusting me with real ownership from day one. I was also fortunate to work alongside such an exceptional team who set high standards and pushed me to grow quickly. VoxSmart gave me exposure to complex enterprise environments, demanding buyers, and high-bar execution. I’ll be carrying all these earned lessons forward in my new chapter. 131 reactions · 53 comments 131 · 53 comments Post Obrigado, Brazil 🇧🇷❤️ What started as an idea between myself, Calum Douglas and Umar Syed in our London office — backed by the trust of our CEO, Oliver Blower — became a fully executed go-to-market campaign in Brazil. Built from scratch. Delivered in-market. And entirely in Brazilian Portuguese… We don’t speak the language. But we made the cold calls. Held the meetings. Ran the sessions. And proved that VoxSmart will go further than anyone to show up for its clients. This wasn’t a branding trip. It was strategy, translated into action. We discovered real compliance challenges on the ground — and delivered real solutions in the room. The market is ready, and we were there to meet it. VoxSmart isn’t new to LATAM. We’ve been building here for years. We’re the most trusted name in comms surveillance and mobile compliance in Brazil — and we’re setting the pace for where the market goes next. If you haven’t engaged with us yet, now’s the time. This is what thinking locally and acting globally really looks like 🫡 #VoxSmart #ObrigadoBrazil #MobileCompliance #WhatsAppSurveillance #RegTech #LATAM #SãoPaulo #ComplianceLeadership 101 reactions · 21 comments 101 · 21 comments

About Adrian built a professional foundation in interior architecture, UX/UI design, and product development. With over a decade of experience designing digital platforms, brands, and immersive experiences across Germany and the US, he approaches technology with the same artistry he brings to music, creating spaces that move people emotionally and intellectually. 4 years ago, his exploration of blockchain technology and decentralization sparked the idea for Sound Vista: the first immersive AI and onchain social streaming platform for music and media. Today, Adrian merges his artistic and entrepreneurial paths to help shape the next generation of streaming, music, media, and internet experiences. As a music producer and DJ, Adrian released a series of EPs and a full-length album that reflect his unique sonic identity, blending emotive soundscapes with forward-thinking rhythms. His work showcases a deep connection between sound, storytelling, and cultural exploration. Adrian has performed internationally, playing DJ sets that merge global sounds with electronic music. Learn more at: soundvista.netlify.app ash.komi.io … more

A big part of that time is spent discovering music -- because imagine: the same tracks, every weekend. It would get boring fast, for both DJs and the crowd. We live in a time where music isn’t scarce anymore, but with so much access, discovery hasn’t become easier; it’s become more fragmented. Multiple platforms, tabs, and personal notes, it quickly turns into 💥 cognitive overload. 💥 ohr brings that experience back into one place -- by making connections between artists, labels, and scenes visible and explorable. Not just to make it easier, but to reflect what electronic music has always been about: connection and exchange. We launched our MVP last week, and it’s been really special to see the first people exploring it. If you’re curious, you can check it out at ohr.fm. About Senior Software Engineer specializing in scalable backend architectures and mission-critical financial system migrations. I have over 4 years of experience building revenue-generating products that serve millions of users. My expertise lies in Golang, Python, and System Design, with a proven track record of solving complex data integrity issues in high-transaction environments. Key Architectural Achievements: Identity-Merge Migration: At Park+, I led a high-stakes migration to eliminate dealer duplication and fraud. I architected a non-mutative mapping layer to unify entities while preserving the integrity of ₹5Cr+ monthly transaction volume. Frictionless Enterprise Approvals: Engineered an asynchronous Email Approval Channel at Procol supporting both Outlook Graph API and IMAP (Gmail). This enabled C-suite stakeholders to execute high-value approvals directly from their inbox without platform authentication. Performance Engineering: Optimized microservices to achieve API response times of <250ms and utilized multithreading to migrate 500k+ images in one hour (a 48x speed improvement). System Scalability: Designed a multi-tenant pass system (B2B/B2C) from the ground up using SOLID principles and Design Patterns (Strategy & Factory). This reduced development lead time for new integrations by 70%. Core Stack: Golang (Gin), Python (Django), Java, Ruby on Rails, MySQL, Postgres, Redis, Kafka, ElasticSearch, Kubernetes, Docker, AWS. … more

This recognition reflects my approach to backend engineering — curiosity-driven learning, actionable problem-solving, and enabling others to succeed. Excited to keep pushing boundaries, learning fast, and contributing beyond my scope. Activity 287 followers Anand Nair reposted this The Nobel Prize 1,166,491 followers 6mo • Edited • BREAKING NEWS The Royal Swedish Academy of Sciences has decided to award the 2025 #NobelPrize in Physics to John Clarke, Michel H. Devoret and John M. Martinis “for the discovery of macroscopic quantum mechanical tunnelling and energy quantisation in an electric circuit.” This year’s physics laureates’ experiments on a chip revealed quantum physics in action. A major question in physics is the maximum size of a system that can demonstrate quantum mechanical effects. The 2025 physics laureates conducted experiments with an electrical circuit in which they demonstrated both quantum mechanical tunnelling and quantised energy levels in a system big enough to be held in the hand. Quantum mechanics allows a particle to move straight through a barrier, using a process called tunnelling. As soon as large numbers of particles are involved, quantum mechanical effects usually become insignificant. The laureates’ experiments demonstrated that quantum mechanical properties can be made concrete on a macroscopic scale. In 1984 and 1985, John Clarke, Michel H. Devoret and John M. Martinis conducted a series of experiments with an electronic circuit built of superconductors, components that can conduct a current with no electrical resistance. In the circuit, the superconducting components were separated by a thin layer of non-conductive material, a setup known as a Josephson junction. By refining and measuring all the various properties of their circuit, they were able to control and explore the phenomena that arose when they passed a current through it. Together, the charged particles moving through the superconductor comprised a system that behaved as if they were a single particle that filled the entire circuit. This macroscopic particle-like system is initially in a state in which current flows without any voltage. The system is trapped in this state, as if behind a barrier that it

About I’m a Data Scientist and Machine Learning Engineer with a strong background in computational astrophysics, high-performance computing, and modern ML/GenAI workflows. I design and ship end-to-end solutions, from data pipelines and model development to MLOps and cloud deployment on AWS. I work comfortably both independently and in cross-functional teams, clarifying ambiguous problems and driving them to measurable results under tight time constraints. I build deep learning and classical ML systems with PyTorch and Scikit-learn and take them from experimentation to reliable production using Docker, Kubernetes, CI/CD, and HPC infrastructure. My recent work includes real-time space-weather forecasting pipelines and ML-accelerated turbulence modeling for binary neutron star (BNS) merger simulations that cut compute costs by up to 8×. I enjoy turning complex technical challenges into clear, well-engineered solutions that create real impact. … more Top skills Machine Learning • Data Science • Generative AI • Artificial Intelligence (AI) • High Performance Computing (HPC)

Led the 2024 Mother's Day Solar Storm case study within the international PAGER project, coordinating multi-institutional analysis of the forecasting pipeline. Result's presented at ESWW 2024 and EGU 2025. 2. Developed and upgraded the SWIFT forecasting package, building real-time pipelines combining MHD numerical simulations and ML models with MLOps practices on HPC clusters to deliver 2–3 day solar storm and aurora predictions for satellite operators. … more Software Development, High Performance Computing (HPC) and +9 skills Researcher : Turbulence Modeling University of Potsdam · Full-time Jan 2023 - Sep 2024 · 1 yr 9 mos Potsdam, Brandenburg, Germany · On-site Turbulence Modeling for Numerical Relativity Simulations of Binary Neutron Star Mergers : Turbulence in binary neutron star mergers remains an open problem in computational astrophysics — global simulations cannot resolve small-scale turbulent dynamics due to the extreme computational costs involved. To help address this, I implemented an adaptive GRLES (General Relativistic Large Eddy Simulation) turbulence model within the BAM numerical relativity code. The model is designed to respond dynamically to local flow conditions and grid resolution, capturing subgrid-scale turbulence while keeping computational costs manageable. I calibrated the subgrid viscosity against high-resolution simulation data, which enabled lower-resolution simulations to more faithfully reproduce fine-scale turbulent behavior. Using this framework, I carried out large-scale simulations on HPC clusters (requiring over 2 million CPU hours) to investigate how turbulence influences neutron star merger dynamics, including its effects on gravitational wave emission and mass ejection. section_map.Recommendations: Recommendations Recommend Ashwin Received (1) Given James Edmond · 2nd Physicist pivoting to data science with expertise in statistical analysis and machine learning. Skilled in handling complex datasets and developing data-driven solutions to solve real-world business problems July 10, 2025, James managed Ashwin directly I am a postdoctoral researcher at section 1.5 at GFZ-Potsdam, the group responsible for providing data products for magnetospheric forecasting (GFZ Radiation Belts Forecast) using physics and ML-driven models. Ashwin has worked under my supervision as an assistant researcher, and has been indispensable in organizing and maintaining our CI/CD pipelines, managing Docker-oriented workflows, and handling large-scale datasets (100’s GBs to ~TBs). Working in this environment requires proficiency in deep learning frameworks like TensorFlow and PyTorch as well as core ML libraries like XGBoost and scikit-learn, and Ashwin has helped ensure robust, production-grade solutions. Part of his work has been to maintain upstream forecast software (SWIFT, an MHD code) from our partners at the University of Warwick, demonstrating excellent teamwork and cross-institutional coordination. In aspects of HPC environments, ML Ops practices, DL frameworks, and collaborative problem-solving, Ashwin has proven to be highly capable and reliable. I give him my strongest recommendation for industry roles demanding these skills and am confident he will excel. Please reach out to me if further information is needed. … more

About Albert Einstein once paraphrased a wonderful quote regarding Math as - 'Mathematics is the Kingpin of Scientific Thinking!' Inspired with the belief that mathematics can be the better way to approach any problem, Leena adapted herself to understand & experiment with different knowledge extracting tools of Machine learning & Artificial Intelligence which are not any magical toolbox rather the concise mathematical functions formulated to represent the Data in the simpler way. Leena likes exploring new technologies! She is acquiring domain knowledge & expertise while working on varied Machine Learning & Reinforcement learning projects using Bayesian probabilistic Inferences & Markov Decision Process applied in Finance domain & different POCs about Generative AI applications in varied domains. She is also working on challenging NLP projects on Image-Audio-Video processing for animal species detection (Faunawatch NGO as a novice Naturalist & Philomathean!) Skills defining Leena: • Mathematical understanding of any model. Naturally she tend to first try to focus on actual simpler Mathematical explanation of any model. • Analyzing & observing or thinking how data will flow through model while implementing or designing any ML model for particular application. • Leena's passion and curiosity to learn and adapt her problem solving skills through different experiments and approaches. Surprising fact about Machine Learning Engineer diversity- 88% MLE are Men while only 12% MLE are Women. Happy to be the one contributing in those 12% MLE workforce! section_map.Projects: Projects AI - Bayesian Probabilistic Inferences Show project Aim: Probabilistic searching of cells with different rules to find target cell successfully. Algorithmic Thinking: We are restricting our cell search movements to choosing a neighbor cell of the current position with different rules. We are using 4 agents and deciding the cell surfing based upon 4 different rules. Agent 1: Travel to the nearest cell chosen by highest probability of containing target and search it. Agent 2: Travel to the nearest cell chosen by by highest probability of finding target and search it. Agent 3: At each time, score each cell with (manhatten distance from current location)/(Probability of finding the target in that cell) Agent 4: At each time, take the average score of 3 cells with (manhatten distance from current location)/(Probability of finding the target in that cell) Key Rule: Understanding the difference between highest probability of containing target vs highest probability of Finding target using Bayesian Inferences. … more Data Visualization, Possess strong analytical and +6 skills AI - Developing a New Heuristic - Relaxation Technique Associated with Rutgers University–New Brunswick Show project Aim: Tried to develop a new heuristics based upon a relaxation strategy and tried to observe the working of the Astar algorithm in higher dimensions. Algorithmic Thinking: 1. Create original Maze with a given dimension(dim) and blockage probability (p) 2. Convert the original dim dimension maze into a sufficiently smaller dimensions but make sure to retain its original blockage properties 3. Design a method to retain the newly formed superblocks original blockage properties 4. Find out the optimal path in the relaxed version of the maze 5. Implement the Astar in original maze with the nodes of the optimal path of its relaxed version 6. Observe the performance accuracy … more Possess strong analytical, Algorithm Development and +1 skill Show all

profiles.about: About Albert Einstein once paraphrased a wonderful quote regarding Math as - 'Mathematics is the Kingpin of Scientific Thinking!' Inspired with the belief that mathematics can be the better way to approach any problem, Leena adapted herself to understand & experiment with different knowledge extracting tools of Machine learning & Artificial Intelligence which are not any magical toolbox rather the concise mathematical functions formulated to represent the Data in the simpler way. Leena likes exploring new technologies! She is acquiring domain knowledge & expertise while working on varied Machine Learning & Reinforcement learning projects using Bayesian probabilistic Inferences & Markov Decision Process applied in Finance domain & different POCs about Generative AI applications in varied domains. She is also working on challenging NLP projects on Image-Audio-Video processing for animal species detection (Faunawatch NGO as a novice Naturalist & Philomathean!) Skills defining Leena: • Mathematical understanding of any model. Naturally she tend to first try to focus on actual simpler Mathematical explanation of any model. • Analyzing & observing or thinking how data will flow through model while implementing or designing any ML model for particular application. • Leena's passion and curiosity to learn and adapt her problem solving skills through different experiments and approaches. Surprising fact about Machine Learning Engineer diversity- 88% MLE are Men while only 12% MLE are Women. Happy to be the one contributing in those 12% MLE workforce! Entrepreneurship - Leena was the First Woman Entrepreneur of her College to build & profitably ran an e-commerce business (www.indfest.com) with free home delivery of eco-friendly materials during Indian festival seasons in Nashik, Lead team of 11 people. Leena loves Trekking, Traveling, Origami, Event Organizing, Programming - Algorithmic Thinking! Join Leena's GenAI Self Exploration journey via different Cloud Services - Excited to have brainstorming discussions regarding various challenging projects! About I am a Computer Science Junior with a strong focus on real-time systems, AI integration, and full-stack development. My experience spans Python, JavaScript, React, Flask, and Java, with applied work in speech-to-text, image captioning, translation, and OCR. I have worked with state-of-the-art models such as Whisper and Qwen-VL and understand how to deploy them effectively in production environments. I am especially skilled at debugging complex systems, recognizing patterns, and building multilingual, accessible applications. My interests lie in the intersection of AI and usability, with a focus on creating scalable and inclusive solutions. I enjoy working on projects that combine backend intelligence with seamless frontend interaction. … more

Featured Post ✨I’m thrilled to share that Team Dristi won Third Prize at the Nexus Technology Cup 2025, held at The Estuary in Baton Rouge from June 17–19!✨ This incredible multi-day event gave us the opportunity to meet inspiring innovators, showcase our AI-powered accessibility app, and gain valuable insights from a panel of experienced judges and industry leaders. Out of more than 70 submissions, only 21 teams were selected for live demonstrations and we’re incredibly grateful to have been one of them. Our app, Dristi, is designed to assist visually impaired users by providing real-time scene descriptions using AI, promoting accessibility and independence through a fully voice-controlled experience. A heartfelt thank you to Nexus Louisiana for creating such an empowering platform for student entrepreneurs and tech innovators to shine. And a huge shoutout to Tony Zanders, Paul Barrial and Na’Tisha Natt for making this opportunity possible! Your support, mentorship, and vision made this an unforgettable experience. I’m also deeply thankful to our coach Mikala Streeter for guiding us at every step of this journey, and especially to my amazing teammate Unika Bista and mentor Dr. Prasanthi Shreekumari! None of this would have been possible without you. This is only the beginning, and we can't wait to see where Dristi goes next! 💡 #NexusTechnologyCup #Dristi #AIForAccessibility #TechForGood #StudentInnovation #VoiceTechnology #WomenInTech #NexusLouisiana 56 reactions · 16 comments 56 · 16 comments Post ✨ Thrilled to share that our project “Dristi” won 2nd place at the Hawkathon 2025 this weekend out of 15 teams!✨ Dristi, meaning “vision,” is an AI-powered voice-guided app created to empower blind and visually impaired individuals. It helps users understand their surroundings through real-time audio descriptions, without the need to look at or touch the screen. I’m grateful to GDSC ULM and ACM Student Chapter at ULM for organizing such a thoughtful and impactful hackat
